intro.h1: Why this exists — and how to take part.
intro.lede:
storytree is more than a tool — it's a bet about how software gets built once AI agents
        write most of the code. If that bet resonates, here's where we are, how we try to keep
        ourselves honest, what we commit to, and how to come in.

bet.h2: Proof is a stronger foundation for trust than promises.
bet.lede:
Most projects ask you to trust the people running them. We're trying to show you don't have
          to. When agents write most of the code and humans can't read all of it, the thing that keeps
          software honest isn't good intentions — it's proof: work that has to pass a check it can't
          talk its way past before it counts as done. Build that in, and trustworthy software becomes
          something far more people can take part in making. If that works, it's a fairer way to build.
          If it doesn't, you'll be able to see that too.

honest.h2: We claim only what's actually built.
honest.body:
The fastest way for a project like this to lose you is to describe a finished product that
        isn't there yet. So we don't. Everything here is meant to be either working today or clearly
        marked as where we're headed — and where there's a gap between the two, we say so plainly
        rather than imply a wall that isn't built yet. Inside storytree, that discipline is the same
        one the product enforces on everyone: work only counts as done when a check that was failing
        is made to pass, and not by the same agent that wrote the check — so nothing grades its own
        homework.
honest.muted:
You don't have to take that on faith, either. You're welcome to watch, to ask hard questions,
        and to tell us where we're wrong — that last one especially. If something rings hollow, this
        project is built to hear it.

constitution.body:
What storytree believes, and how it plans to keep itself honest, lives in one place — written
        plainly. It's a living draft; it'll change as we learn. But it's the thing to hold us to.

where.body:
storytree is small on purpose. Taking part is invite-only while we iterate out the rough
        edges and build the guardrails this needs. Today one person still decides what gets built
        next — we're honest that "your voice counts" isn't yet "your vote counts." But this website is
        the open front door: you're welcome to watch, to ask hard questions, and to tell us where
        we're wrong — that last one especially.
